section_name: Operation of Motor Vehicle in Violation of Financial Responsibility Requirement; Offense
text:
(a) A person commits an offense if the person, while in the course and scope of the person’s employment with a valet parking service, operates a motor vehicle of a patron of the service without the financial responsibility required by this chapter.

(b) Except as provided by Subsections (c) and (d), an offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $175 or more than $350.

(c) If a person has been previously convicted of an offense under this section, an offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $350 or more than $1,000.

(d) If the court determines that a person who has not been previously convicted of an offense under this section is economically unable to pay the fine, the court may reduce the fine to not less than $175.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 686. Valet Parking Svcs.
##### § 686.006 — Operation of Motor Vehicle in Violation of Financial Responsibility Requirement; Offense

(a) A person commits an offense if the person, while in the course and scope of the person’s employment with a valet parking service, operates a motor vehicle of a patron of the service without the financial responsibility required by this chapter.

(b) Except as provided by Subsections (c) and (d), an offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $175 or more than $350.

(c) If a person has been previously convicted of an offense under this section, an offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $350 or more than $1,000.

(d) If the court determines that a person who has not been previously convicted of an offense under this section is economically unable to pay the fine, the court may reduce the fine to not less than $175.

*Added by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 816, Sec. 23.002, eff. March 1, 2004.*

subsections.label: b

subsections.label: c

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: If the court determines that a person who has not been previously convicted of an offense under this section is economically unable to pay the fine, the court may reduce the fine to not less than $175.
